publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-09-16 18:22:07 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Forest Hills Gardens Corporation (Forest Hills, New York, N.Y.)

did.physdesc: Correspondence, minutes, reports, memoranda, vouchers, orders, notices, clippings, specifications, and other materials.
did.abstract: Collection includes materials relating to the history of Forest Hills Gardens, a planned residential community in Queens, New York that was devleloped by the Russel Sage Foundation in 1908.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: Forest Hills Gardens is a planned residential community which was developed by the Russell Sage Foundation in 1908. Laid out on 175 acres in Queens, New York, the community's site plan was designed by Frederick Law Olmsted; many of the houses were designed by Grosvenor Atterbury.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection includes correspondence, minutes, reports, memoranda, vouchers, orders, notices, clippings, specifications, and other materials pertaining to Forest Hills Gardens meetings, committees, director, constitution and by-laws, insurance and liability, finance, maintenance, development, construction, zoning, maintaining architectural standards and aesthetic control, building restrictions and waivers, streets and parking, complaints, policies, the sale of property to "undesirables," the Ives-Quinn anti-discrimination bill, and other subjects. Also included are reports of the Architectural Committee and correspondence with the Russell Sage Foundation, the Sage Foundation Homes Co., Forest Hills Dwellings, Inc., residents, and the New York City Planning Commission.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIAL
relatedmaterial.p: See also Forest Hills Gardens (New York, N.Y.) Records, ( #2495).

odd.head: General
odd.p: Materials in Box 28, where noted, pertain to the development of the Vanderveer Tract, which adjoins Forest Hills Gardens and over which FHG has architectural control as per agreement of May 17, 1923

emph.ref: Series I. Alphabetical Files and Other Correspondence
Series II. Vouchers, Permits, and Legal Documents
Series III. Maps, Plans, Oversized Items, Brochures, and Glass Plate Negatives

altformavail.head: Available Copies:
altformavail.p: Corporate minutes, 1922-1958 are on microfilm. Originals retained by donor.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Forest Hills Gardens Corporation Records, #2775. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access to microfilm of corporate minutes restricted to the permission of the donor.

controlaccess.geogname: Forest Hills Gardens (New York, N.Y.) -- Buildings.
controlaccess.subject: Zoning.
Urban transportation.
Discrimination in housing.
Community development, Urban.
City planning -- New York (N.Y.)
controlaccess.corpname: Forest Hills Gardens Corporation. Architectural Committee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lists and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Forest Hills Garden Corp. regarding suggestions in matters of "Aesthetic Control" (4 pages)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding transferral of rights and obligations from the Homes Company to an as yet unnamed Corporation; copy of Roland Park (Baltimore, MD) Corporation Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Especially Atterbury

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Men's Club lists and correspondence; additionally, excerpts from FHG Bulletin 1915-1923 (four typed pages) and 1928 correspondence especially regarding Block 12 controversy (apt. house)

did.unittitle: Forest Hills Gardens Bulletins - including Christmas Number December 13, 1924

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding politics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Olmstead Brothers (landscape architects) acknowledging receipt of Certificate of Incorporation and By-Laws of Gardens Corp. (1 page)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Atterbury letter regarding architectural supervision

did.unittitle: Adsit Reports - Office diary by Mrs. Adsit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes newspaper clippings regarding barring of 2 family homes in Union Turnpike (by Bloodgood)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Especially with Newton Gas Co. regarding permission to open streets at various locations

did.unittitle: Orders - FHGC office orders especially addressograph plates

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copies of printed notices sent to lot owners contemplating building regarding restrictions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memo affecting Union Turnpike frontage (2 pages)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Between "A" property owners and FHGC regarding development and maintenance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter from DWSG & E regarding lighting of private streets (city does not supply); correspondence regarding development of P.S. 101

did.unittitle: Judge Boyce - list of delinquent maintenance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between Cord Meyer Development Co. and FHGC regarding Sewer Pumping Station; misc. "C" correspondence especially Chatford Homes Development Corp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of proposed agreement (unsigned) between FHGC and Forest Hills Dwellings, Inc. regarding wavier to permit houses to stand closer to street (95 houses on Burns St.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Between "E" property owners and FHGC regarding development and maintenance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report of "Four Mill Committee" to FHGC - read and approved and adopted at directors meeting July 3, 1924, (members to pay Four mills tax for maintenance and nothing for dues) - agreement to run with the land (formerly agreement was 2 mills tax, 2 mills membership dues); also copy of earlier Four Mill Agreement dated October 1, 1923 , (raising from 2 mills the assignment from the Homes Company)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding damages and repairs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence regarding "Eagle" (Safety) Patrol

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes of directors' and open meetings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding development and maintenance charges between property owners and FHGC; letter from NYC Fire Dept. denying request for fire alarm system, (i.e. boxes in our streets)

did.unittitle: Newton Register Printing - Correspondence with printing company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports on private lanes, private parks, Olivia and Hawthorne Parks, underground improvements, especially regarding taxes and maintenance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proof copies of printing jobs, e.g.: FH Bulletin, copy of assessment agreement regarding 4 mill maintenance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly in architectural matters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Sprunk vs. FHGC regarding unauthorized building

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Real Estate Tax levies for 1923 and other tax matters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Electric (for installations and current for particular lamps in FHG, numbering 1 to 4 locations) and telephone

scopecontent.p: 1923-1924, including copy of History and Constitution

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bills and receipts (inc. some 1923-1925)

scopecontent.p: Includes Laura Green Vouchers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of Four-Mill Agreement dated Oct. 1, 1923

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including one Atterbury letter

scopecontent.p: Including Electric Service Agreement for 8 Mazda Type lamps in FHG - also permits for installation of these

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Knott Corp. Lease 1942; Notice regarding stakes in curb plots; correspondence regarding owners infringements in Gardens property; Telephone Company-Sage Foundations Homes Co. Agreement dated February 20, 1913 (original copy, regarding easements, rights of way); owners petition for bench in Flagpole park October 9, 1940; Report of Committee in Proposed Membership Corporation of Property Owners to take over control maintenance and restrictions at F.H.G. dated October 24, 1922; Certificate of Incorporation of Gardens Corporation December 7, 1922; Indenture January 1, 1923, between Sage Foundation Homes Co. and Russell Sage Foundation and Gardens Corp. (Gardens Corp. assumes rights and duties of Homes Company);Office memo regarding operating procedures; Agreement between Chapford Homes Corp. and Victor Palsits and Gardens Corporation (regarding property construction); contract for painting of flagpole, photocopy of letter from IRS regarding tax exempt status of FHGC dated as received November 11, 1942; Forest Hills Gardens Taxpayers Assoc. Constitution and By-Laws (no date); Certificate of Change of Name of Gardens Corp. to Forest Hills Gardens Corporation November 10, 1939; Constitution and By-Laws of Women's Club of Forest Hills (carbon copies); application for dump permit, dump permit for 1948; also permit for 1945, 1941, 1944, 1946; dump truck vehicles registration 1943, 1944, 1955, 1948; gasoline ration card for dump truck 1942

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Orders and correspondence with vendors, information, publicity regarding changes in Forest Hills Inn, redecoration, etc.

did.unittitle: Captain Gerald MacDonald - A Biographical Memorial

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Leather bound volume prepared for MacDonald Family and privately printed by the American Historical Society, Inc. N.Y. 1930. Former Forest Hills resident (house may have been on site of present Kennedy House on Queens Boulevard)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A collection of early views of Forest Hills Gardens, 1913 to approximately 1920, primarily of the Forest Hills Inn, Station Square and Flag-pole Green at the time of the July 4th celebrations. Observance of this Holiday was very important and was a day long event beginning with a sunrise proclamation by a Town-crier dress in costume (see photo) and continuing with a parade, games, dances, speeches, and a flag raising ceremony at Flag-pole Green

The Perry Collection #3442, 7-74 case 3, has a box with a collection of 37 lantern slides used by Mr. Perry for lectures on city planning Aug. 1, 1946. Fourteen of these slides are of Forest Hills Gardens, showing early views of Station Square dated July 1914 and of the July 4, 1914 celebration (similar to #28-41 in Box 30, #2775). Also, included is an early landscape overview of the Terraces (and Block 12) looking east from the Forest Hills Inn and an early view of the Olivia Park. A slide of an entrance gate to the Gardens is also included

did.unittitle: 2-27 - Spectators, games, races, flag raising ceremonies in Flag-pole Green on occasion of 4th of July celebration earlier - some Atterbury row houses visible on Greenway Terrances
28-41 - July 4th celebration observances in Station Square and Forest Hills Inn area - speaker shown in #28 and #29, may be Teddy Roosevelt, a frequent visitor to Forest Hills Gardens
42-72 - Young dancing girls performing outdoors in July 4th celebration events
77 - View from Burns Street towards Square
78 - Early view from Terraces into Station Square ca.
84 - Street lantern affixed to Forest Hills Inn in Station Square area
85 - Lantern light fixture near Forest Hills Inn in Station Square area
86 - Greenway Terrace group of row houses (Atterbury)
91 - Atterbury house corner of Greenway North and Overhill Road
92 - Early grandstands at West Side Tennis Club before tennis stadium was built
93 - Early photo of West Side Tennis Club before the stadium was built
